properties.name: title
properties.value: The Hype Cycle and Trough of Disillusionment

concepts.title: Strong vs Weak Technologies
concepts.content:
## The iPhone Moment

When the iPhone launched in 2007, critics dismissed it. The wireless network was too slow. Users were accustomed to physical keyboards. There were no apps. Calls dropped frequently. Yet Steve Jobs smiled when a journalist complained about the touchscreen keyboard and simply said: **'Your thumbs will learn.'**

The iPhone was so compelling that the world adapted to it — carriers built out wireless capacity, developers created new apps, users learned touchscreen typing. This is the essence of **strong technology**.

## The Pattern

Technologies usually emerge in pairs: a **strong form** and a **weak form**.

| Strong (reshapes world) | Weak (adapts to world) |
|------------------------|------------------------|
| Public internet | Private intranets |
| iPhone (full OS) | Blackberry (keyboard) |
| Permissionless blockchain | Private blockchain |
| Full electric vehicles | Hybrid cars |
| VR | AR |

## Why Weak Tech Feels Safer

Weak technologies appear more practical to incumbents. They're familiar, productive, serious, safe. Strong technologies look strange, toy-like, unserious, expensive, even dangerous. So organizations choose the weak compromise.

## The Long Game

Strong technologies captivate enthusiasts — weekend hobbyists, not paid employees. Enthusiasts think long-term, design from first principles, and vote with their time. When the mainstream world finally notices, it's often too late to catch up.

**Your thumbs will learn.** The world adapts to strong technologies, not the other way around.

concepts.title: The Hype Cycle and Trough of Disillusionment
concepts.content:
## The Five Phases

Strong technologies often follow the Perez/Gartner hype cycle:

```
Innovation → Peak of Inflated → Trough of → Slope of → Plateau of
Trigger      Expectations      Disillusionment   Enlightenment   Productivity
```

## The Critical Moment: The Trough

The **trough of disillusionment** is where strong technologies are tested. After the initial hype fades, reality sets in:
- The technology doesn't work as promised yet
- Mainstream adoption seems distant
- Investors lose patience
- Critics say 'I told you so'

This is when entrepreneurs who bet on strong tech often lose faith and pivot to weak tech. **This is usually a mistake.**

## Why Weak Tech Looks Better in the Trough

Weak technologies appear closer to mainstream adoption because they don't require the world to change. A Blackberry with a better keyboard seems more achievable than convincing everyone to learn touchscreen typing.

But weak tech rarely defines the future.

## Historical Examples

**Public Internet (Strong)** went through the trough in the early 1990s. Companies built private intranets instead (weak). By 2000, the public internet won.

**Blockchain (Strong)** hit the trough in 2018-2019. Enterprises built private blockchains (weak). Public blockchains survived and define the space today.

## The Lesson

If you're building strong tech and hit the trough, don't pivot to weak tech. The trough is not proof of failure — it's a phase. Strong technologies that survive the trough become the technologies that define eras.

concepts.title: The Unreasonable Man
concepts.content:
## George Bernard Shaw's Insight

> 'The reasonable man adapts himself to the world: the unreasonable one persists in trying to adapt the world to himself. Therefore all progress depends on the unreasonable man.'

This is the philosophical core of strong vs weak technologies.

## Two Approaches to Innovation

| Approach | Philosophy | Example |
|----------|-----------|--------|
| **Reasonable (Weak)** | Adapt to current world | Blackberry kept keyboards because users knew them |
| **Unreasonable (Strong)** | Make world adapt | iPhone removed keyboard, said 'your thumbs will learn' |

## Why Strong Tech Requires Unreasonableness

Strong technologies demand changes:
- iPhone: Carriers had to build out wireless networks
- iPhone: Developers had to learn new paradigms
- iPhone: Users had to relearn typing

A 'reasonable' person would say: 'This is too much to ask. Just make a better keyboard phone.' That's the weak tech path.

## The Entrepreneurial Choice

When you're in the trough of disillusionment:
- **Reasonable:** Pivot to weak tech that fits the current world
- **Unreasonable:** Persist in making the world adapt

## Progress Requires Unreasonableness

Every transformative technology required unreasonable persistence:
- **Tesla:** Electric cars when everyone said they'd never work
- **Bitcoin:** Digital currency when everyone said it needed government backing
- **SpaceX:** Reusable rockets when NASA said it was impossible

The reasonable people adapt. The unreasonable people shape the future.

quizzes.question: Spotify (streaming music) vs iTunes (MP3 downloads). Which is the strong technology?
options: iTunes — it digitized the existing model of buying individual albums and tracks
Spotify — it required the world to adapt (always-on internet, subscription model)
Both are equally strong since they both disrupted the music industry's physical sales
Neither qualifies as strong technology since the music industry drove both changes
quizzes.correctAnswer: Spotify — it required the world to adapt (always-on internet, subscription model)

quizzes.question: Blockchain hit a 'trough of disillusionment' around 2018-2019. What was the weak technology pivot many enterprises made?
options: Doubled down on public blockchain development with reduced feature sets
Adopted hybrid architectures that bridged public chains with traditional databases
Shifted investment to layer-2 scaling solutions on existing public chains

quizzes.question: Tesla insisted on full electric vehicles when the industry said hybrids were more practical. Which approach did Tesla take?
options: Reasonable — adapted to what the industry accepted by starting with luxury hybrids first
Unreasonable — made the world adapt to their vision
Compromise — built a phased roadmap that included both hybrid and electric models
Incremental — improved existing combustion technology with gradual electrification
quizzes.correctAnswer: Unreasonable — made the world adapt to their vision
